strategy_summary: The binary is a 64-bit ELF with NX enabled and no PIE. The vulnerable function reads 512 bytes into a 128-byte buffer, causing a stack overflow. We can use this to overwrite the return address. Since there is no RELRO, we can overwrite the GOT entries. We will use the 'pop rdi; ret' gadget to control the first argument of 'system' and then call it to get a shell. We need to measure the offset to the return address and find the address of 'system' in the GOT.
